findings.evidence: `rg -n "@\[expose\]" code/TauCeti/Probability/Exchangeability/PathSpace` hits `Cylinder.lean:40`, `Cylinder.lean:59`, `ProcessShift.lean:41`, `ProcessShift.lean:59`, and `ProcessShift.lean:77`; the same files export `mem_prefixCylinder`, `indProd_apply`, `processShift_apply`, `processCons_zero`, `processCons_succ`, and `processTail_apply`.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` from `prefixCylinder`, `indProd`, `processShift`, `processCons`, and `processTail`; keep the existing public simp/apply lemmas, and add any missing characteristic lemma rather than exposing bodies.
findings.issue: All five new public definitions are marked `@[expose]`, making their bodies part of the public interface although consumers can use the exported `mem_*`/`*_apply`/interaction lemmas instead.

summary: The new API exposes all definition bodies even though the files already provide public characteristic lemmas for the needed reductions. This broadens the public surface unnecessarily and should be replaced by lemma-based API.
